extraction.article_title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Axios Hack, Chrome 0-Day, Fortinet Exploits, Paragon Spyware and More

ttps.description: Exploitation of publicly exposed software supply chain and internet-facing services to gain initial access or distribute malicious updates at scale.
Compromise of software dependencies and package distribution channels to deliver malicious code to downstream consumers.
Compromise of supply chain components and trusted developer tooling to abuse software trust relationships.

proposed_phase_updates: Monitor package registry integrity and maintainer account security for sudden version changes.
Add CI/CD controls to verify dependency provenance, signatures, and publication alerts.
Detect unexpected post-install or runtime behavior in newly published package versions.

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: A trusted package is replaced or updated with code that executes when imported, installed, or invoked by a downstream application, causing arbitrary command execution in the consumer environment.
proposed_kb_patterns.payload: module.exports = (() => { const { execSync } = require('child_process'); execSync('sh -c "id > /tmp/pwned"'); return {}; })();
detect: command execution telemetry from node or npm lifecycle scripts
new network or process activity immediately after dependency update
